truths.statement: OweMe deliberately does not gate basic functionality: every plan (including Free) supports complete debt management, and higher tiers only unlock sophistication (more staff, deeper analytics, integrations, higher AI, support), never core recovery workflows.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.57-58 ("Why We Do Not Sell Features")

truths.statement: The Automation Engine (automatic reminder scheduling, dashboard updates, balance calculations, receipt generation, overdue detection, daily summaries, weekly reports, sync) is available to every customer regardless of subscription tier.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.7 p.41 ("Automation Engine")

truths.statement: The Automatic Reminder Engine is a core platform feature available from the very first version of the product, not a premium add-on.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.6 p.31 ("Automatic Reminder Engine")

truths.statement: The Free Starter plan retains core recovery workflows: customer management, debt recording, receipt generation, offline support, and a basic dashboard.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.56-57 ("Starter / Free")

truths.statement: AI is treated as a premium, metered capability rather than an unlimited resource; every plan carries a monthly AI credit allowance and overage requires purchasing AI credit bundles.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.6 p.33 ("AI as a Premium Resource") / ch.9 p.60 ("AI Credits")

truths.statement: Pricing is a hybrid of three dimensions: fixed subscription, Business Scale (BVUM), and infrastructure-usage allowances/top-ups for AI, SMS and WhatsApp.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.56 ("The Hybrid Pricing Model")

truths.statement: Four plans exist: Starter (Free), Market (₦2,000/month), Business (₦5,000/month), and Enterprise (starting from ₦15,000/month).
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.56-58 ("Subscription Plans")

truths.statement: BVUM ceilings per plan: Market up to ₦2 Million BVUM, Business up to ₦20 Million BVUM, Enterprise unlimited BVUM (Starter has no BVUM ceiling stated).
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.57-58 (plan cards)

truths.statement: Staff-account limits per plan: Market 1 staff account, Business up to 5 staff accounts, Enterprise unlimited staff (Starter lists no staff account).
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.57-58 (plan cards)

truths.statement: Monthly AI credits per plan: Starter 10, Market 100, Business 500, Enterprise Fair Usage.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.60 ("AI Credits" table)

truths.statement: The Free Starter plan is capped at up to 10 automatic reminders.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.56 ("Starter / Free" includes list)

truths.statement: BVUM (Business Value Under Management) is OweMe's internal business-scale measure, computed with illustrative weights: Outstanding Receivables 40%, Monthly Credit Issued 30%, Recovery Volume 15%, Active Debtors 10%, Business Complexity 5%; the formula is internal and may evolve.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.59 ("BVUM Calculation")

truths.statement: The automatic reminder schedule uses fixed offsets relative to the due date: 3 days before due date, on the due date, 3 days overdue, and 7 days overdue, after which the cycle stops when the debt is recovered.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.6 p.31 ("Automatic Reminder Engine") / ch.7 p.40 ("Automatic Reminder Scheduling")

truths.statement: The company North Star metric is Total Value Recovered Through OweMe, explicitly not revenue, downloads, or social metrics.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.11 p.69 ("The OweMe North Star Metric")

truths.statement: A debt progresses through defined stages: Draft → Active → Upcoming → Due Today → Overdue → Partially Paid → Recovered → Cancelled.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.7 p.38 ("Debt Status")

truths.statement: The reminder cycle is created automatically the moment a debt is recorded, and stops automatically once payment/recovery occurs (business rule: "Stop reminders after payment").
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.6 p.30-31 / ch.7 p.41 ("Business Rules Engine")

truths.statement: Plan upgrades follow: business activity raises BVUM → system observes consistency over a 30-day observation period → upgrade recommendation → the customer must confirm the upgrade (no silent auto-upgrade on a single large transaction).
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.60 ("Plan Upgrades")

truths.statement: Plan downgrades are only recommended after BVUM stays below a plan threshold for an extended period; temporary slow months must not force an immediate downgrade.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.60 ("Plan Downgrades")

truths.statement: The deterministic Business Rules Engine is authoritative; AI never controls business-critical workflows, never makes irreversible financial decisions, and its recommendations are always optional and user-editable.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.8 p.53 ("AI Service") / ch.13 p.83 ("Artificial Intelligence Risks")

truths.statement: Deterministic operations—balance calculation, overdue determination, reminder scheduling, payment application, receipt numbering, recovery-percentage calculation, and structured search—must use traditional software and must never be delegated to AI.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.6 p.32-33 ("Where AI Should Never Be Used") / ch.8 p.49 ("Business Rules Engine")

truths.statement: Email is not a primary/core communication channel; primary reminder channels are SMS, WhatsApp, and printable statement/reminder letters (with future assisted calls), and email is supported only as an optional capability.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.6 p.32 ("Reminder Channels") / ch.7 p.41 / ch.8 p.51 ("Why Email Is Not Core")

truths.statement: Push notifications are exclusively for the business owner and are never sent to debtors; debtors are never expected to install OweMe.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.6 p.32 ("Internal Notifications") / ch.7 p.44 ("Business Notifications")

truths.statement: Offline-first / local-first: every important action (customer creation, debt recording, payment recording, receipt generation, reminder scheduling, search, analytics viewing) completes on the local device immediately, with cloud synchronization happening later without user interaction.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.8 p.46-50 ("Architecture Philosophy", "Local Database", "Synchronization Layer") / ch.7 p.44 ("Offline Capability")

truths.statement: Partial payments automatically update the remaining outstanding balance; no manual balance calculation is ever required of the user.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.7 p.38-39 ("Partial Payments")

truths.statement: Reminder automation itself is unlimited, but the cost-bearing delivery channels (SMS and WhatsApp) operate on monthly allowances with optional purchasable bundles.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.61 ("Messaging")

truths.statement: The system is event-driven: recording a payment emits an event cascade—debt updated → analytics updated → receipt generated → dashboard updated → business notification sent—with no service directly coupled to another.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.8 p.53 ("Event Processing")

truths.statement: Reminder tone/policy is business-configurable at the account level, with named styles such as Gentle Reminder, Friendly Follow-up, and Final Reminder.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.7 p.40 ("Reminder Policies")

truths.statement: Reminder entitlement is contradictory: the doc states automation/reminders are "available to every customer regardless of subscription tier" and a core feature "from the very first version," yet the Free Starter plan caps automatic reminders at 10 and the pricing chapter says "automatic reminders remain part of every paid plan" (implying the free tier is excluded).
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.7 p.41 / ch.6 p.31 vs ch.9 p.56 (Starter cap) & ch.9 p.61 ("remain part of every paid plan")

truths.statement: Plan names are inconsistent across the document: the pricing chapter defines plans as Starter / Market / Business / Enterprise, but the metrics chapter references upgrades "from Market to Shop or Wholesale," plan names that appear nowhere in the pricing table.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.56-58 (plan cards) vs ch.11 p.69 ("Net Revenue Retention")

truths.statement: The Market plan lists "Unlimited reminders*" with an asterisk whose footnote is never defined at the plan table; only the later Messaging section implies the caveat is the SMS/WhatsApp monthly allowance, leaving the intended reminder-vs-delivery limit ambiguous.
truths.provenance: OweMe.pdf ch.9 p.57 (Market card) vs ch.9 p.61 ("Messaging")
